subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Cosmic Web
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Cosmic Web.

hook.text: Galaxies aren't scattered randomly. They form filaments, walls, and clusters—a vast web stretching across the cosmos.

buildup.text: Dark matter acts as scaffolding. Galaxies formed along dense filaments, leaving enormous voids between them.

discovery.text: Computer simulations match the observed web perfectly. The pattern was set by quantum wobbles in the infant universe.

twist.text: The largest voids are hundreds of millions of light-years across—nearly empty of galaxies. Cosmic deserts.

climax.text: Mapping the cosmic web reveals the universe's largest structures and confirms how gravity shapes everything.

punchline.text: Galaxies on threads. Voids between them. The universe has architecture.

quiz.question: What role does dark matter play in the cosmic web?
options: It acts as invisible scaffolding along which galaxies formed
It fills the voids between galaxy filaments
It repels galaxies to create the web pattern
